items.caption:
A slide from a presentation on the topic of radioactive decay and radioactive isotope dating. it contains text and illustrations related to these scientific concepts. the slide is divided into two main sections: "radioactive decay" and "radioactive isotope dating."

under "radioactive decay," there are bullet points explaining that radioactive isotopes are unstable and decay into particles with lower atomic numbers. it mentions that radioactive isotopes are used in medicine and tracers are taken up by the body, detected using special lab equipment. the slide also states that the rate of decay of a radioactive element is constant and that the amount of decay can be used to date fossils.

the "radioactive isotope dating" section provides a diagram illustrating the decay of carbon-14 (14c) into nitrogen-14 (14n). the diagram shows the decay process with arrows indicating the transformation of 14c into 14n, accompanied by a half-life of 5,730 years. the slide also includes a picture of a rabbit, which is likely used as an example of an organism that can be dated using radioactive isotope dating.

the slide is educational and informative, designed to explain complex scientific concepts in a clear and concise manner. the use of illustrations and diagrams helps to visualize the abstract concepts of radioactive decay and isotope dating.
